pattern: Enumerating every contiguous subarray sum, sorting that multiset, and summing a positional slice reduces an odd-sounding statistic to plain array bookkeeping.
hints: There are exactly n*(n+1)/2 non-empty contiguous subarrays; every one of their sums can be listed by extending a running total for each start index.
Once you have all subarray sums in a list, sort it in non-decreasing order - the problem is now just 'sum a 1-indexed inclusive slice of a sorted array'.

Build the sums with a double loop (start index, then extend the end index while accumulating a running total to avoid recomputing sums from scratch), sort, then add sums[left-1 .. right-1] and reduce modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: single-element array, only one possible subarray sum
all-equal values with a mid-range slice
left=right=1 picks only the smallest subarray sum
left=right=n*(n+1)/2 picks only the largest subarray sum (the whole-array sum)
whole valid range must include every subarray sum exactly once, not clip the last one
a narrow one-wide window in the middle exercises the off-by-one between left-1 and left

stress.description: random arrays up to the maximum length with a random valid [left, right] window
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    total = n * (n + 1) // 2
    left = rng.randint(1, total)
    right = rng.randint(left, total)
    return [nums, n, left, right]
